verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like fold ectodomain) that is expressed exclusively on mature sperm cells, where it localizes to the sperm surface and is essential for sperm-oocyte plasma membrane fusion. It has its own TM domain and is genuinely surface-displayed, but only in the highly restricted germline/gamete lineage (mature spermatozoa). Per the triage rules, a TM protein with bona fide outer-leaflet display restricted to a narrow developmental/gamete lineage maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface, not yes/classical_surface_receptor.
